description: Original tasks before rewriting based on actual data

tasks.name: Bookmark three weekend tree-planting events in June 2025 within 25 miles
tasks.description: Find local tree-planting events happening on Saturdays or Sundays in June 2025 within 25 miles of ZIP code 94110, and bookmark exactly three of them to your saved events list.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click on the main navigation menu item labeled "Events".
In the events page, locate the ZIP/postal code field in the filters sidebar and type "94110".
Find the distance or radius filter and set the maximum distance to "25 miles" using the slider or input field.
Open the date filter, select the month "June 2025", and apply the filter.
In the event type/category filter, select the checkbox labeled "Tree planting" (or "Tree Planting Day" if present).

Use any available day-of-week or schedule filter to show only events labeled as happening on "Saturday" or "Sunday"; if there is no such filter, scroll the June 2025 results and focus only on events that show "Sat" or "Sun" in their date line.
Sort the filtered events by "Date - Soonest First" using the sort dropdown.

From the top of the resulting list, open the first three events that meet the weekend and distance criteria by clicking each event title, and on each event detail page click the "Bookmark" or "Save to My Events" button once.

tasks.name: Create a low-cost conservation action plan with 5 high-impact actions
tasks.description: Use the action planner to build a personal conservation plan that includes exactly 5 actions, each with an estimated monthly cost of $50 or less and an impact score of at least 3 out of 5, then save the plan with a specific name.
steps: From the homepage, click on the navigation link labeled "Action Planner" or "My Plan".
On the action planner page, click the "Browse Actions" or "Add Actions" button to open the action library.
In the cost filter section, set the maximum estimated monthly cost to "$50" using the slider or input box, and make sure no minimum cost is set higher than "$0".
In the impact or effectiveness filter, select only actions with an impact rating of "3 stars" or higher (or an impact score of "3/5" or greater).
Use the sort dropdown to sort the filtered actions by "Impact - High to Low".
From the top of the sorted list, click the "Add to Plan" button on the first 5 actions that appear.
After adding 5 actions, click the "View My Plan" or "Review Plan" link to open your plan overview.
In the plan name field at the top of the overview, type "Low-Cost High-Impact Plan".
Click the "Save Plan" or "Save Changes" button to store this 5-action plan under that name.

tasks.name: Set a carbon footprint reduction goal of at least 20% for a 3-person household
tasks.description: Use the carbon calculator for a 3-person household, generate a baseline annual carbon footprint, then create and save a reduction goal that targets at least a 20% decrease from the baseline.
steps: From the homepage, click on the "Tools" menu and select "Carbon Footprint Calculator".
On the calculator start screen, find the household size field and enter "3" as the number of people.
Enter "2" in the field for number of bedrooms (or select the closest option if only ranges are available).
In the transportation section, enter a weekly driving distance of "150" miles and select a vehicle fuel type that best matches a typical gasoline car (e.g., "Gasoline").
In the energy section, enter an average monthly electricity use of "400" kWh and select "Natural gas" as the heating type with an average monthly usage of "30" therms if the field exists.
Click the button labeled "Calculate" or "View Results" to generate the baseline annual carbon footprint, then click the "Set Reduction Goal" or similar button on the results page.
In the reduction goal section, move the reduction percentage slider or enter a numeric value so that the reduction percentage is at least "20%" (e.g., type "20" in the percent field).
In the goal name field, type "20% Reduction for 3-Person Home".
Click the "Save Goal" or "Add to My Goals" button to store this reduction goal with the calculator profile.

tasks.name: Start a monthly wildlife conservation pledge under $30 with high transparency
tasks.description: Select a wildlife conservation project that has a transparency rating of at least 4 out of 5 and a default suggested monthly donation of $30 or less, then set up a recurring monthly pledge of $25 to that project using the on-site pledge form.
steps: From the homepage, click on the main navigation link labeled "Donate" or "Support Projects".
On the projects page, use the category filter to select the "Wildlife" or "Wildlife Conservation" category only.
In the transparency or accountability filter, select only projects with a rating of "4" or more out of "5" (e.g., tick checkboxes for 4-star and 5-star transparency).

Use the cost or suggested donation filter to show projects with a suggested monthly donation of "$30" or less, if such a filter exists; otherwise, use the sort dropdown to sort by "Suggested Monthly - Low to High".
From the filtered list, click on the first project that meets the wildlife category and high transparency criteria to open its detail page.
On the project detail page, locate the donation frequency options and select "Monthly" as the donation type.
In the amount input field, type "25" to set the monthly pledge to $25.
Fill in the name field with "Jordan Green" and the email field with "jordan.green@example.com" in the pledge form.
Click the "Start Monthly Pledge" or "Confirm Monthly Donation" button to submit the $25 recurring pledge to this project.

tasks.name: Register for an evening ocean conservation webinar within the next 60 days
tasks.description: Find an ocean conservation webinar scheduled to start after 5:00 PM local time within the next 60 days and register for it, adding it to your on-site events dashboard.
steps: Navigate to the homepage and click on the "Events" or "Programs" menu item.
On the events page, use the event type filter to select "Webinars" or "Online Events" only.
In the topic or theme filter, select "Oceans" or "Ocean Conservation".
Open the date range filter and set the start date to today and the end date to a date 60 days from today, then apply the filter.
If a time-of-day filter is available, select "Evening" or set the start time filter to "after 5:00 PM"; if not, scroll the results and focus on webinars whose listed start time is later than 5:00 PM.
Use the sort dropdown to sort the filtered webinars by "Date - Soonest First".
Click on the first webinar that matches the ocean topic and evening start time to open its detail page.
On the webinar detail page, click the "Register" or "Sign Up" button.

In the registration form, enter "Taylor Rivers" in the name field and "taylor.rivers@example.com" in the email field, then select the option labeled "Add to My Events" or "Add to Dashboard" before clicking the final "Submit" or "Complete Registration" button.

tasks.name: Save four recent short plastic pollution articles to a reading list
tasks.description: Search for beginner-friendly articles about plastic pollution published in the last 2 years with an estimated reading time of 10 minutes or less, and add exactly four of them to a new reading list.
steps: From the homepage, click on the "Learn" or "Articles" section in the main navigation.
In the search bar on the articles page, type "plastic pollution basics" and press Enter to run the search.
Open the date filter and set it to show only articles published within the last "2 years".
Locate the reading time filter and set the maximum reading time to "10 minutes".
In the difficulty or level filter, select the "Beginner" or "Introductory" level.
Use the sort dropdown to sort the results by "Most Recent".
Starting from the top of the sorted results, click the bookmark or "Add to List" icon on the first 4 articles that match these filters.
Click the "My Reading Lists" or "Reading List" link in the header or sidebar to open your saved items area.

Create a new reading list by entering the name "Plastic Pollution Starters" in the list name field and adding those 4 selected articles to this list using the available "Add to List" or drag-and-drop controls.

tasks.name: Join a local wildlife volunteer group within 10 miles with 4+ star reviews
tasks.description: Find and join a local wildlife-focused volunteer group that is located within 10 miles of ZIP code 30309 and has an average user rating of at least 4 out of 5 stars, then set your preferred participation frequency to weekly.
steps: From the homepage, click on the "Volunteer" or "Get Involved" menu item.
On the volunteer opportunities page, switch to the "Groups" or "Ongoing Groups" tab if there is one.
In the location filter, enter ZIP code "30309" and set the maximum distance or radius to "10 miles".
In the cause or focus area filter, select "Wildlife" or "Wildlife Habitat" as the primary focus.
Use the rating filter to include only groups with an average rating of "4 stars" or higher.
Sort the filtered results by "Rating - High to Low" using the sort dropdown.
Click on the first group in the results list that matches the wildlife focus and rating criteria to open its detail page.
On the group detail page, click the "Join Group" or "Sign Up" button.
In the preferences section or follow-up form, select "Weekly" from the participation frequency dropdown and click "Save Preferences" or "Confirm".

tasks.name: Activate three easy daily energy-saving challenges from the lifestyle quiz
tasks.description: Complete the lifestyle quiz and, based on the results, activate at least three daily challenges that are labeled as 'Easy' difficulty and belong to the 'Energy Saving' category.
steps: On the homepage, click on the "Tools" or "Challenges" menu and select the "Lifestyle Quiz" option.
On the quiz start page, click the "Start Quiz" button.

For each quiz section (such as Home Energy, Transportation, and Waste), select answers that best describe a typical average household; for example, choose options indicating moderate energy use rather than very low or very high where such choices are presented.
After answering all quiz questions, click the "See My Results" or "View Recommendations" button.
On the results page, locate the recommendations or challenges section and filter the suggested challenges by category to show only "Energy Saving".
Use the difficulty filter to select only challenges labeled "Easy".
From the filtered list, click the "Activate" or "Add to My Challenges" button for the first 3 daily challenges displayed.
Navigate to the "My Challenges" or "Dashboard" area via the header or sidebar link to view your active challenges list.

If there is an option to set cadence, ensure each of the 3 new challenges is set to a "Daily" frequency by selecting "Daily" from any available frequency dropdown next to each challenge and clicking a "Save" or "Update" button for the list.
